name: add-emacs
tagline: Wires Emacs as a chat channel for the NanoClaw assistant via a local HTTP bridge, copying adapter files from a branch and configuring.
verdict: Clean and well-built integration skill: no exfiltration, no piped-shell or obfuscated payloads, all network calls are localhost-only, idempotent steps, and thorough troubleshooting/failure handling. Weak spots are packaging hygiene: no version/changelog signal, no explicit scope exclusions against sibling channel skills, an unverifiable REMOVE.md reference.

highlights.summary: No 'NOT for'/out-of-scope language anywhere, despite clearly being one of a family of add-X-channel skills.
Line 302 links 'See [REMOVE.md](REMOVE.md) to uninstall' but no file inventory was provided to confirm it exists.
pnpm/vitest/tsx (lines 61-62, 99) are invoked with no upfront requirements/availability check, though implied by the host project.
